1990_08_29-India-Mumbai-SB_8_7_19-Spiritual_Life_Means_Warfare-st2-vr02

Event Name/Title

Spiritual Life Means Warfare

Event Date

29 Aug 1990

Event Location

India, Mumbai

Event Type

Temple - SB class

Transcriber

Lokeshwar Krishna Das

Completed

12 Aug 2021

Source Audio

https://www.radhanathswamimedia.com/Lec/1990/90-076-Spiritual%20Life%20Means%20Warfare%20(Mumbai)-SB_8_7_19-dt_90_08_29.mp3

Source Video

Verse

SB 08.07.02-19

Tags – Places

Click here to enter text.

Tags – Pastimes

Ocean of Milk churning

Tags – Persons

Lord Ajita, Demigods, Demons, Madara Mountain, Vasuki

Tags – Themes

Association, Mercy, Love of God, Anarthas, Chanting

Lecture Transcription

hariḥ purastāj jagṛhe
pūrvaṁ devās tato ’bhavan

[SB 8.7.2]

[The Personality of Godhead, Ajita, grasped the front portion of the snake, and then the demigods followed.]

tan naicchan daitya-patayo
mahā-puruṣa-ceṣṭitam
na gṛhṇīmo vayaṁ puccham
aher aṅgam amaṅgalam
svādhyāya-śruta-sampannāḥ
prakhyātā janma-karmabhiḥ

[SB 8.7.3]

[The leaders of the demons thought it unwise to hold the tail, the inauspicious portion of the snake. Instead, they wanted to hold the front, which had been taken by the [Supreme] Personality of Godhead I am sorry I am reading may be wrong…[break]]

upary adhaś cātmani gotra-netrayoḥ
pareṇa te prāviśatā samedhitāḥ
mamanthur abdhiṁ tarasā madotkaṭā
mahādriṇā kṣobhita-nakra-cakram

[SB 8.7.13]

[The demigods and demons worked almost madly for the nectar, encouraged by the Lord, who was above and below the mountain and who had entered the demigods, the demons, Vāsuki and the mountain itself. Because of the strength of the demigods and demons, the Ocean of Milk was so powerfully agitated that all the alligators in the water were very much perturbed. Nonetheless the churning of the ocean continued in this way.]

ahīndra-sāhasra-kaṭhora-dṛṅ-mukha-
śvāsāgni-dhūmāhata-varcaso ’surāḥ
pauloma-kāleya-balīlvalādayo
davāgni-dagdhāḥ saralā ivābhavan

[SB 8.7.14]

[Vāsuki had thousands of eyes and mouths. From his mouths he breathed smoke and blazing fire, which affected the demons, headed by Pauloma, Kāleya, Bali and Ilvala. Thus the demons, who appeared like sarala trees burned by a forest fire, gradually became powerless.]

devāṁś ca tac-chvāsa-śikhā-hata-prabhān
dhūmrāmbara-srag-vara-kaṣcukānanān
samabhyavarṣan bhagavad-vaśā ghanā
vavuḥ samudrormy-upagūḍha-vāyavaḥ

[SB 8.7.15]

[Because the demigods were also affected by the blazing breath of Vāsuki, their bodily lusters diminished, and their garments, garlands, weapons and faces were blackened by [the] smoke. However, by the grace of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, clouds appeared on the sea, pouring torrents of rain, and breezes blew, carrying particles of water from the sea waves, to give relief to the demigods.]

mathyamānāt tathā sindhor
devāsura-varūtha-paiḥ
yadā sudhā na jāyeta
nirmamanthājitaḥ svayam

[SB 8.7.16]

[When nectar did not come from the Ocean of Milk, despite so much endeavor by the best of the demigods and demons, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Ajita, personally began to churn the ocean.]

megha-śyāmaḥ kanaka-paridhiḥ karṇa-vidyota-vidyun
mūrdhni bhrājad-vilulita-kacaḥ srag-dharo rakta-netraḥ
jaitrair dorbhir jagad-abhaya-dair dandaśūkaṁ gṛhītvā
mathnan mathnā pratigirir ivāśobhatātho dhṛtādriḥ

[SB 8.7.17]

[The Lord appeared like a blackish cloud. He was dressed with yellow garments, His earrings shone on His ears like lightning, and His hair spread over His shoulders. He wore a garland of flowers, and His eyes were pinkish. With His strong, glorious arms, which award fearlessness throughout the universe, He took hold of Vāsuki and began churning the ocean, using Mandara Mountain as a churning rod. When engaged in this way, the Lord appeared like a beautifully situated mountain named Indranīla.]

nirmathyamānād udadher abhūd viṣaṁ
maholbaṇaṁ hālahalāhvam agrataḥ
sambhrānta-mīnonmakarāhi-kacchapāt
timi-dvipa-grāha-timiṅgilākulāt

[SB 8.7.18]

[The fish, sharks, tortoises and snakes were most agitated and perturbed. The entire ocean became turbulent, and even the large aquatic animals like whales, water elephants, crocodiles and timiṅgila fish [large whales that can swallow small whales] came to the surface. While the ocean was being churned in this way, it first produced a fiercely dangerous poison called hālahala.]

tad ugra-vegaṁ diśi diśy upary adho
visarpad utsarpad asahyam aprati
bhītāḥ prajā dudruvur aṅga seśvarā
arakṣyamāṇāḥ śaraṇaṁ sadāśivam

[SB 8.7.19]

[O King, when that uncontrollable poison was forcefully spreading up and down in all directions, all the demigods, along with the Lord Himself, approached Lord Śiva [Sadāśiva]. Feeling unsheltered and very much afraid, they sought shelter of him.]

Purport

One may question that since the Supreme Personality of Godhead was personally present, why did He accompany all the demigods and people in general to take shelter of Lord Sadāśiva instead of intervening Himself. In this connection Śrīla Madhvācārya warns:

rudrasya yaśaso ’rthāya
svayaṁ viṣṇur viṣaṁ vibhuḥ
na saṣjahre samartho ’pi
vāyuṁ coce praśāntaye

Lord Viṣṇu was competent to rectify the situation, but in order to give credit to Lord Śiva, who later drank all the poison and kept it in his neck, Lord Viṣṇu did not take action.

Borrowed property of Krishna

In the process of churning of ocean of milk we find that such a task was impossible, even by the most learned, the strongest of all living beings within the universe. We find that at every stage the help of the Supreme Personality of Godhead was actually required. We are never the doers of our activities. Whatever we have is the borrowed property of Krishna that is clearly illustrated in this verse.

Churning of Milk Ocean

Now we come to the subject, why are they churning the ocean of Milk? They are performing this great service which is also a very very difficult sacrifice, for the purpose of producing nectar. But, actually, are they producing nectar? The nectar is already there. The process of churning just separates the essence from whatever else is present. Just like when you churn the butter… the butter milk, what takes place is-- what appears to be just one homogeneous liquid, cream. Through the process of churning you separate the essence, the butter from that which is mixed within the butter and due to that mixture the real essence of butter cannot be seen. So the pro…process of churning is the process of separating the essence from anything else that may be present, bringing about the essence that is already there but it’s hidden. Now the churning of the ocean of milk is similar. Within the ocean is nectar because it is mixed with rest of the ocean, it is diluted in this way. The real life giving nectars must be somehow or other extracted.

Now, here, the greatest of all personalities within the universe they cannot do it themselves. No matter how harder they tried, despite all the vast learning, despite all the good karma, despite all their physical prowess, despite all their managerial abilities, they cannot extract the nectar from the ocean. Therefore the Lord Himself as Kurma rupa was at the bottom. In the form of the thousand armed incarnation, He was at the top. In the form of the trigunas, the guna avatar, He appeared in the trigunas. He appeared within the demigods and demons on either side. In the form of Ajita, He Himself did the churning, until He actually took the serpent Himself and started churning. Nothing was happening to the ocean.

To become Krishna Conscious

Now to become Krishna conscious is churning the ocean of our consciousness. At this particular time the essence, bhakti, pure devotion is within our consciousness.

Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu has said,

nitya-siddha kṛṣṇa-prema ‘sādhya’ kabhu naya
śravaṇādi-śuddha-citte karaye udaya

[CC Madhya 22.107]

[Pure love for Kṛṣṇa is eternally established in the hearts of the living entities. It is not something to be gained from another source. When the heart is purified by hearing and chanting, this love naturally awakens.]

That pure love of God is within everyone’s heart. But the problem is our Bhakti, our devotion is utterly diluted and mixed by so many material desires, so many unwanted things and anarthas and therefore this Bhakti must be separated. Our real consciousness, our pure natural consciousness must be separated but from the illusion that is surrounding it in all directions. And therefore sadhana bhakti is the process of churning of our consciousness. Now when we first start churning it creates great agitation. Just like, we see here as the demigods and the demons are churning the ocean of milk the alligators, the timingila fish, the sharks and all the other aquatics within that ocean have become very much disturbed, very agitated and they do not like this churning. So similarly when we start churning our consciousness through the process of sadhana bhakti all of our material desires become very fearful. All of our material desires become very much disturbed and agitated. Why? it is explained…[14.09-14-11 break] If you want to defeat a tiger, you have to stop feeding him. The more you feed a tiger the more powerful and hungry and the more powerful and controlling he becomes. But if you stop giving food to the tiger he becomes very disturbed because he is not getting what he normally gets as a routine. Therefore his mind and his senses become very ferocious and if you respond to his roaring demands then you simply go back to the beginning point of dealing with all powerful tiger. But if you can somehow or other tolerate all of his roaring and screaming and all of his agitation, gradually the tiger becomes weaker and weaker and weaker until he dies. So the controlling of the mind is the same way. When we take to the sadhana bhakti, vaiddhi bhakti, we begin to churn our consciousness and the first thing that happens is our material desires that are dormant within our heart because they have not becoming fulfilled, they become very much disturbed and very agitated and what happens they start coming to the surface. what you doing, stop this, we cannot be disturbed. Also we must know that by our own abilities, we cannot extract the nectar because our consciousness, like the ocean of milk, is so vast that it is beyond our capabilities and beyond the capability of all the help of everyone within this world to extract the nectar from our consciousness.

Mercy of Guru and Krishna

It is only by, tad-vijṣānārthaṁ sa gurum evābhigacchet [Muṇḍaka Upaniṣad 1.2.12], tasmād guruṁ prapadyeta
jijṣāsuḥ śreya uttamam[SB 11.3.21]. It is only by mercy of Krishna or He especially empowered representative of the spiritual master, with their help that the process of viddhi bhakti can be properly followed.

rāga-dveṣa-vimuktais tu
viṣayān indriyaiś caran
ātma-vaśyair vidheyātmā
prasādam adhigacchati

[Bg 2.64]

[But a person free from all attachment and aversion and able to control his senses through regulative principles of freedom can obtain the complete mercy of the Lord.]

Krishna says in Gita that by following the regulative principles which is vaiddhi bhakti, one can attain the complete mercy of the Lord and thus one can become free from all attractions and aversions. In the same way the demigods and the demons, simply they had to try. It was beyond them. It was impossible for them but simply they were trying as hard as they could, ananyāś cintayanto māṁ ye janāḥ paryupāsate [Bg 9.22]. If you try very hard, Krishna comes and He helps. He preserves what you have, He carries what you lack. If you simply observe the regulative principles to the best of your ability, however difficult and impossible that may be, you attract the mercy of the Lord and by the mercy of the Lord everything is possible.

mūkaṁ karoti vācālaṁ
paṅguṁ laṅghayate girim
yat-kṛpā tam ahaṁ vande
śrī-guruṁ dīna-tāraṇam
[paramānanda-mādhavam]

[CC. Madhya 17.80]

[The Supreme Personality of Godhead has the form of sac-cid-ānanda-vigraha — transcendental bliss, knowledge and eternity. I offer my respectful obeisances unto Him, who turns the dumb into eloquent speakers and enables the lame to cross mountains. Such is the mercy of the Lord.]

The Lord’s mercy is coming through Gurudeva and ones you achieve his mercy nothing is impossible. Even if you are lame, you can cross the mountain. If you are blind, you can see the farthermost distant stars in the sky and even if you are dumb you can speak most beautiful poetry and philosophy. There is nothing impossible for one who attains the mercy of the Lord. Vishnu shakti is all powerful. And if we simply engage, Krishna says, “In following the regulative principles of devotion, which means, we simply try as hard as we can, to perform our service to Gurudeva then you attain the mercy. Krishna comes, He preserves what we have and He carries what we lack”. If the demigods and the demons never agreed to accept the service of churning the ocean, would Vishnu have incarnated in so many ways to help them? Would the nectar have ever been produced? Never. First of all, they surrendered to the service and not only did they surrendered theoretically to the service but also, they gave everything within their power to fulfill the desire of the lord in that service. They worked so hard. They gave every drop of their energy and still it was not near enough. Similarly, if we want to churn the ocean of our consciousness and produce the nectar of prema bhakti, love of God, we must first accept the service of Gurudeva and Krishna and then we must work and try as hard as possibly we can to follow the principles of our guru maharaja’s instructions. And the fact is, it is impossible. The guru is telling us, “Just give up all sense gratification to fix the consciousness always on Krishna.” It is not possible. And as we try we find all these devouring timingili fishes of lust, envy, anger, greed, and all these things that are coming to the surface. We think oh, my God! This is what I am dealing with? It is impossible.

Krishna in Holy Names Churning Anarthas

Krishna is non-different than his name. When we cry out the name, hare kṛṣṇa hare kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa hare hare/hare rāma hare rāma rāma rāma hare hare, the name is non-different than Krishna. The name is non-different than the supreme person, Ajita, who is actually doing the churning. Similarly, the holy name which is the incarnation of Ajita, in this age of Kali yuga. He is the one who takes the churning rod, takes the rope and churns it for us. It is through the sound of the holy name. There are all the ocean begins to become disturbed and agitated. But this is the process of purification and we find that the first thing that comes out of the ocean is all poison, Halahala. It was such a horrible distasteful poison. It is just started spreading all directions and it created fear and havoc amongst all the demigods and the demons because they knew that this poison was so great that it can destroy the entire universe. There was great danger. They are looking for nectar. They are working so hard for nectar. But what they get is poison. Is not that the same thing that takes place in the life of a devotee with chanting our rounds every day, we following the four regulative principles very strictly, we obediently and humbly serving our guru maharaja, at the expense of everything else in our life, giving up our family, our parents think we are crazy. The society thinks we are strange. We have practically no money. We are accepting the position of rejects. Ah?... We are accepting so much inconvenience simply for the purpose of getting this nectar. And what happens? All these poison just started pouring out of our minds. We feel more disturbed, more lusty, more envious, more angry, more greedy, more illusioned, that we ever did before. What’s happening? This is what the demigods and the demons were thinking, you see before the nectar can come out, first of all, all poison has to be extracted and all has to come to the surface that all must be wiped away? So, it is a fact.

Spiritual life means warfare

The darkest moment comes before the dawn. Before you see the sweet nectar, first you have to see everything bad that is within you. when it doesn’t…It is not nice but it’s horrible. Before you understand, what love of God is? First you have to understand how much you do not love God, how many interferences are between you and love of God. Therefore insincere devotee is very bewildered. He is thinking, “I am taking the process to taste nectar and all I am getting is poison”. I will go somewhere else; I will find some spiritual path where I don’t have to churn the ocean of my consciousness. I can just keep the ocean real peaceful as far as possible then I will think, ahha nice nice, everything is very calm. We are thinking this is spiritual life but that is not spiritual life. Spiritual life does not mean peace. Spiritual life means warfare. The real peace comes after the war. As long as the enemy is controlling your camp, how can you find peace? First there has to be war and through that war you have to evacuate the enemy from your camp, then there can be peace. But don’t think that the enemy is just going to leave upon your invitation because they have been holding your mind captive for millions of births very attached.

Just like, we are reading in newspaper about Iraq and Kuwait. The Iraqian… what is his name? ah…? [questioning] Hussain…anyway He is the dictator of Iraq very very powerful personality. He just marched his troops into Kuwait and he took over this country and now that is his and he is not going to give it up to anyone he is fighting. Even the most powerful forces in the world are saying, “Give up Kuwait” and he is saying, “Now it is mine”. aha ha ha…He is only had it for [maharaj make sound] a week or two. Can you imagine if he had it, if it was under their control for millions of years, will they be anxious? They already build their whole civilization on it. Just like when the British came to India they built the VT station and all these nice buildings. They built all these wonderful parliament houses in Delhi. They put so much energy and so much time and so much money and so much man power into this India. When India wanted to get rid of them, they just say, “ohh…Yes yes of course. you don’t want us anymore. hha… Namaste Mahatma Gandhi, I will go”. uh…No, it was not like that. What happened is Gandhiji, Subhash Chandra Bose at different functions, to be… they…they started agitating the situation. They started churning the country. Right? and when they started churning the country the British, Oh, my God! They became very angry; they started cutting people thumbs up. uh? They started killing people. They started brutally beating people. No, Gandhi and Subhash Chandra Bose, would have said, ah…“Too much trouble, too much trouble, let these things become peaceful and let’s just … let’s just wash the feet of the British people then they will be more nice to us”. No, they knew these are the enemies. These people have completely taken over our country. It is ours and they have taken it and they are polluting it and they are misusing it. Therefore they had to produce agitation first and that agitation created a war. There was so much prosecution, so much horrible politics that the whole…[0:26:33] it came to world… hear of the whole world, and this went on for years and years and years. And finally, because they didn’t give up, because they kept churning, and they kept churning with all the poison coming out. Ultimately the enemy had to leave.

So, this is the same process that takes place in spiritual life. As soon as you take to Krishna Consciousness you are declaring war on Maya. Maya has been appearing to be your friend. uh!!! you like me here…I am nice look at all I am providing for you providing so much pleasure, just be alone and you will be happy. But if you anyway try to go against me be prepared for war. So, when we take to the process of following the instructions of the spiritual master, uh… such disturbances you it starts churning the ocean of your mind and it just all the enemy troops start coming to the surface lust, anger, envy, greed, pride, illusion. They all start coming out. All the impurities, all the poison, all the poison starts pouring out and you think ah… unless you are under the direction of a bonafide spiritual master, you will undoubtedly give up. You will think what is happening. You will lose your faith. This is what separates the sincere from the insincere.

Krishna’s Devotees are Glorified

When the poisons start coming out, it’s beyond our control. Therefore what the demigods and demons do when this great halahal poison when came out, they just had to run to the lotus feet of Vishnu. Similarly when this poison just started pouring out we have to take shelter of Krishna. We have to take shelter of the Vani, the words of our spiritual master, the scriptures, and the great sadhus. That is our only hope.

And Lord Vishnu, he is so kind and merciful, very symbolic that Lord Vishnu Himself did not want to accept this most glorious service of drinking the poison. Krishna takes more pleasure in glorifying His devotees than seeing Himself glorified. In the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Krishna could have… Krishna showed the Virat Rupa, the Universal form to Arjuna and He proved to him that, “Actually I can kill all these armies within a second with my flaming mouths. But Arjuna I wanted you to have the credit. I want you to be glorified for the rest of the world’s history. Because a father loves to see his son glorified, even more than himself”. So similarly, here Lord Vishnu whose heart and soul is always captured by the love of Shankara, vaishnavanaam yatha shambho. He is the most pure hearted of all the Vaishnava devotees of the Lord.

In every Vaishnava sampradaya there is the worship of Shiva. We find in Vrindavan, Lord Shiva is worshiped as the Gopeswara Mahadeva, the greatest of all Vaishnavas, the greatest Mahadeva, the greatest of all the demigods, who has taken the form of Gopi to assist Krishna in His divine lila, that is Krishna lila. In Rama Lila we find that lord Shankara is the greatest of all devotees and Rama Himself is worshiping Shankara at Rameswaram and Lord Shankara has manifested in his great expansion incarnation as Hanumanji and playing the role of the greatest of all Vaishnavas and Lord Ramachandra, He has spoken and it is especially traced in the Rama Charita Manas by Tulasi das that unless one worships Lord Shiva, unless one gets the mercy of Hanumanji and Shankara he cannot approach Ramachandra. Not possible. In every holy Dham it is either Hanuman or Shiva, who is the guardian of the Dham. In Badrinath, before going to Badrinath you must first get permission of Hanumanji, just beyond Joshima you have then. In Vrindavan there are Hanuman Mandirs on all the four sides of Vrindavan as there are also temples of Lord Shiva to protect the holy Dham. In Chitrakut, on the top of the high mountain above the chitrakut hill there is Hanuman Dwara, where Hanuman is standing majestically with his club guarding Chitrakut for all time to come.

In Ayodhya there is the most famous temple “Hanuman ksheti” which is on the top of the large mountain and there Hanuman stands guarding all of Ayodhya. In Jagannath Puri we have Bedi Hanuman, where Hanuman is ah… to keep him there as a guard, they have chain his feet but he is always standing their guarding Jagannath Puri from the asuras. In Rameswaram the holy place of Lord Rama, you find that the principal temple is Rameswaram. It is a temple of Lord Shankara.

So, in this way virtually all the holy places of the Vaishnavas they worship Lord Shiva and he always has the most intimate service and you cannot approach the Dham and you cannot approach the Lord without first getting the blessings of Lord Shiva. So, this is how dear Lord Shiva is to the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Therefore he is supremely worshipable. Therefore the Lord wanted to see his devotee getting glorified. So therefore Lord Vishnu, He Himself along with all the demigods and the demons, He directed them, “Let us go to Lord Shankara. He is my devotee. He can save us from this great poison.”

Similarly, when we begin to chant the holy name of the Lord and began to follow of the regulative principles of bhakti and the poisons start coming out. The difficulties start arising within our lives. The war begins. It is said, “By the mercy of Krishna, one gets guru and by the mercy of guru, one gets Krishna”. If we are sincere just as Krishna laid the demons and demigods to the feet of the great param guru Shiva, so similarly if we are sincere in this process of churning our own hearts then Krishna will lead us to the lotus feet of His devotee, the spiritual master. His divine grace will come through Gurudeva and by the mercy of Gurudeva Krishna’s mercy will manifest within our lives. And therefore when we approach the guru maharaja we must glorify the guru maharaja. Because when we glorify the spiritual master, we are giving the greatest pleasure to Krishna. Yes…We sing the glories of our guru maharaja. But the real glorification is to glorify his mission by surrendering to his words.

We chant, we dance, we say jai Prabhupada. That is very nice and that is necessary to come from our hearts. But if it is actually coming through our heart, it is not in there. The real glorification is through sacrifice, through surrender. Your will not my will be done. When we accept the guru’s orders as our life and soul and we are willing to even accept so much difficulty on his behalf. That is the real glorification of the spiritual master and Krishna is so pleased by that glorification. That more than anything else, wins His heart.

sādhavo hṛdayaṁ mahyaṁ
sādhūnāṁ hṛdayaṁ tv aham
mad-anyat te na jānanti
nāhaṁ tebhyo manāg api

[CC. Adi 1.62]

[Saints are My heart, and only I am their hearts. They do not know anyone but Me, and therefore I do not recognize anyone besides them as Mine.]

Lord Narayana has said that the sadhu is My life- My soul because he is always in My heart because I am always in his heart. Because he knows no one but Me and I know no one but him. That is the sadhu. So when we take shelter of the lotus feet of the sadhu which is the greatest gift of Krishna, that He has directed us to such a person. That is the real process of getting the complete mercy of the lord.

Chanting is Churning

So as the poison comes out, do not be afraid, do not be surprised, expect it. It will… it must come out. Before the nectar of love of God arises in your consciousness first all the poison must be extracted. Therefore it is said that the Krishna Consciousness is not cheap. Those who are not willing to tolerate the difficulties and turn to God and guru and the sadhus to help them in this great process in a humble and helpless state of mind. Just continue on… in trying as hard as they can. Some people say, “I cannot chant properly. My chanting, it’s so difficult. My mind is running everywhere. Of course, that’s the poison coming out. When we start the chanting, the chanting is the churning. When you start churning all the poison just starts coming out of your mind. Things that you never knew exist. That is horrible. Sometimes you think, “I am more peaceful when I am not chanting”. The fact that all the poisons come out means that chanting is working. But you have to keep trying. If you are not at all trying to be attentive then chanting will have very little affect. But if you really and seriously trying to be attentive, just like demigods and the demons, as helpless as they are, although they can’t do anything. They are just trying to do it.

Similarly, if we just go on with our chanting, go on with our service, then let that poison be removed let it come out and by the mercy of Krishna, He will empower us. By the mercy of Guru, it is possible. But it is not easy. It will be as difficult as it can be possibly be. It will take you to the limit of all of your own abilities to tolerate. But if you willing to just keep going and keep trying then Krishna says, ah… “This person is serious”. Guru maharaja says, “Yes, yes, this person actually is serious and then they… they take control”. But it does not happen at once. If it happens once, it would be cheap. They let it go on and on and on for sometimes. and then, “Yes, yes now I see this person is really sincere”. And then the poison will be removed and when that poison is removed gradually you can perceive the bhakti, a love within your heart. You see, when the poison is removed, that is mukti that is liberation. But you have to keep churning, even after the poison is removed, before bhava and prema are manifested within your mind.

The Real Bhajana Kriya

Srila Rupa Goswami explains very nicely

ādau śraddhā tataḥ sādhu-
saṅgo ’tha bhajana-kriyā
tato ’nartha-nivṛttiḥ syāt
tato niṣṭhā rucis tataḥ

athāsaktis tato bhāvas
tataḥ premābhyudaṣcati
sādhakānām ayaṁ premṇaḥ
prādurbhāve bhavet kramaḥ

[CC Madhya 23.14-15]

[In the beginning there must be faith. Then one becomes interested in associating with pure devotees. Thereafter one is initiated by the spiritual master and executes the regulative principles under his orders. Thus one is freed from all unwanted habits and becomes firmly fixed in devotional service. Thereafter, one develops taste and attachment. This is the way of sādhana-bhakti, the execution of devotional service according to the regulative principles. Gradually emotions intensify, and finally there is an awakening of love. This is the gradual development of love of Godhead for the devotee interested in Kṛṣṇa consciousness.]

At the beginning there is a little faith. By that faith Krishna responds and sends you to guru. Guru trains you in the principles of sadhana Bhakti. Actually the first thing, I am sorry… first faith…from the faith sadhu sanga. The Lord first of all He gives you the faith based on your desire. Everything is happening by God’s grace, just like the story. You have a little desire for spiritual life, Krishna provides faith. Krishna says in Gita, “I supply all faith”. From that faith he directs you to the association of devotees. What is the association? It is nothing… none other than Krishna Himself manifesting through the sadhus, through their words, through their actions. They are His instruments. So Krishna through sadhus they enlighten you by their words, by their examples and gradually we become inspired and then we start taking to the process of Krishna Consciousness and gradually we accept the initiation by the guru and we surrender to what he tells to not to do and we surrender to what he tell us we should do. That is called bhajana kriya and through the bhajana kriya “anartha nivrtti syat” all the poisons starts coming out. Halada… Halahal starts coming like anything. All the impurities starts come out from this churning process of bhajana kriya. And after all poison is out then nistha, ruchi the higher taste. There is peace of mind. There is liberation. There is the higher taste for Krishna Consciousness. There is pure faith. Now it is upto us to stop the churning as soon as there is liberation uh… and thinking the poison is gone, now everything is good, why should I keep working? Therefore, we need a spiritual master within the Vaishnava sampradaya, who tells us, “No! Continue churning, this is not the end”. Mukti is just the beginning of spiritual life. We keep churning and we keep working, we keep struggling, we keep striving in this process and gradually bhava, love of God awakens and then ultimately prema. Prema means Krishna reveals your eternal relationship with Him in the spiritual world and you can always taste the sweetness of that loving service at every moment of your consciousness. So do not give up this churning process under the able guidance of the guru, the sadhu, and the script…shastra. Continue this great process of churning. Do not be surprised when the poison comes. Krishna is right there to help you. You simply have to give everything within your power to do the best you can in His service. And He will preserve what you have. He will carry what you lack. Krishna says in Gita, “For one who surrenders to Me, I am the swift deliverer from the ocean of birth and death”. Have faith, Krishna will protect you. Continue to fight this great war and know that wherever there is Krishna and Arjuna there is sure to be victory, great opulence, and the perfection of life is guaranteed.

Thank you, very much.

Hare Krishna.
